---
title: "Accuracy and error-rate study - Unconstrained vs. constrained (fact, SLLS)"
date: "Compiled at `r format(Sys.time(), '%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S', tz = 'UTC')` UTC"
output:
  github_document: default
  html_document:
    toc: true
    toc_float: true
always_allow_html: true
params:
  name: "02_constr_vs_unconstr" # change if you rename file
---

```{r here, message=FALSE}
here::i_am(paste0(params$name, ".Rmd"), uuid = "7146734b-7152-4fe6-a555-ed990432b2bd")
```

```{r directories}
# create or *empty* the target directory, used to write this file's data: 
#projthis::proj_create_dir_target(params$name, clean = TRUE)

# function to get path to target directory: path_target("sample.csv")
path_target <- projthis::proj_path_target(params$name)

# function to get path to previous data: path_source("00-import", "sample.csv")
path_source <- projthis::proj_path_source(params$name)
```

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  dev = c("png", "cairo_pdf"),
  fig.ext = c("png", "pdf"),
  fig.width = 10, 
  fig.height = 6, 
  dpi = 200,
  fig.path = paste0('figures/', params, "/"),
  message = FALSE, 
  warning = FALSE,
  echo = FALSE)
```

```{r packages}
library(conflicted)
library(dplyr); conflicts_prefer(dplyr::filter, dplyr::select)
library(tidyr)
library(tibble)
library(purrr)
library(stringr)
library(ggplot2)
library(patchwork)
library(progressr)
library(future)
library(furrr)
library(readr)
library(rlang) # For global plot theme
library(knitr)
library(kableExtra)

conflicts_prefer(base::intersect, dplyr::rename, base::as.factor)
```

```{r}
# Global parameters
cores <- 8
```


## Load permApprox functions

```{r permapprox-load}
# Load permApprox functions
proj_path <- rprojroot::find_root(rprojroot::has_file("README.md"))
permApprox_path <- file.path(proj_path, "functions/permApprox_eps_fct/R")
permApprox_functions <- list.files(permApprox_path, pattern = "\\.R$", full.names = TRUE)
invisible(sapply(permApprox_functions, source))
```

## Epsilon rule

```{r eps-rule, echo=TRUE}
# Build T_cap from choice: "perm" or "tmax"
# - cap_base = "tmax": uses observed maximum across tests
# - cap_base = "perm": uses high quantile of |perm_stats| inflated by g(n)
.build_Tcap <- function(cap_base, t_obs, perm_stats, n, 
                        q_ref = 0.99, alpha = 0.5) {
  if (identical(cap_base, "tmax")) {
    # Observed cap (same for all tests)
    T_cap <- rep(max(abs(t_obs), na.rm = TRUE), length(t_obs))

  } else if (identical(cap_base, "perm")) {
    # Permutation cap: high quantile of |perm|, inflated by g(n)
    if (is.null(dim(perm_stats))) perm_stats <- matrix(perm_stats, ncol = 1)
    Tabs <- abs(perm_stats)
    Tq <- apply(Tabs, 2, stats::quantile, probs = q_ref, na.rm = TRUE, names = FALSE)
    
    # Inflation g(n) = max(1, alpha * sqrt(n))
    infl <- pmax(1, alpha * sqrt(n))
    T_cap <- Tq * infl

    # Broadcast if needed
    if (length(T_cap) == 1L) T_cap <- rep(T_cap, length(t_obs))
    if (length(T_cap) != length(t_obs)) T_cap <- rep(T_cap[1L], length(t_obs))

  } else {
    stop("cap_base must be 'perm' or 'tmax'.")
  }

  # Guardrails: enforce positive finite caps
  bad <- !is.finite(T_cap) | T_cap <= 0
  if (any(bad)) {
    fallback <- max(T_cap[!bad], 1)
    T_cap[bad] <- fallback
  }
  T_cap
}

# Standardized Lifted Log-Saturation (SLLS) using unified Z-cap
# - Standardize to Z using permutation mean/sd per test
# - Build the cap on the Z-scale via .build_Tcap (cap_base = "perm" or "tmax")
# - Apply simplified LLS on Z
# - Map epsilon back to T-scale via sigma_j
eps_slls <- function(obs_stats,
                     perm_stats,
                     sampsize,
                     cap_base      = "tmax",  # Z-cap choice
                     alpha         = 0.5, # g(n) = max(1, alpha*sqrt(n))
                     q_ref         = 0.99, # quantile of |Z_perm|
                     rho_lift      = 3,
                     k_factor      = 1000,
                     target_factor = 0.25,
                     floor_epsZ    = 1e-6,
                     ...) {
  cap_base <- match.arg(cap_base, choices = c("perm", "tmax"))
  n <- sampsize
  stopifnot(n > 0)
  
  # Shape inputs --------------------------------------------------------------
  # Allow vector perm_stats for single test
  if (is.null(dim(perm_stats))) perm_stats <- matrix(perm_stats, ncol = 1)
  Tobs <- as.numeric(obs_stats)
  
  # Z-standardization per test -----------------------------------------------
  mu  <- colMeans(perm_stats, na.rm = TRUE)
  sig <- apply(perm_stats, 2, sd, na.rm = TRUE)
  
  # Guard against non-finite or zero SDs: replace with a small positive fallback
  valid_sig <- sig[is.finite(sig) & sig > 0]
  sig_fallback <- if (length(valid_sig)) max(min(valid_sig), 1e-12) else 1e-12
  sig_ok <- sig
  sig_ok[!is.finite(sig_ok) | sig_ok <= 0] <- sig_fallback

  Zobs  <- abs((Tobs - mu) / sig_ok)
  Zperm <- sweep(sweep(perm_stats, 2, mu, "-"), 2, sig_ok, "/")  # same shape as perm_stats

  # Build Z-cap (unified) -----------------------------------------------------
  # Uses: "tmax" => max(Zobs); "perm" => Q_q(|Zperm|) * g(n) with g(n)=max(1, alpha*sqrt(n))
  T_cap_Z <- .build_Tcap(cap_base = cap_base,
                         t_obs    = Zobs,
                         perm_stats = Zperm,
                         n        = n,
                         q_ref    = q_ref,
                         alpha    = alpha)

  # LLS core on Z -------------------------------------------------------------
  k        <- k_factor      * (500 / n)
  E_target <- target_factor * (500 / n)

  if (!any(is.finite(T_cap_Z)) || all(T_cap_Z <= 0)) {
    # Conservative constant if the cap failed
    epsZ <- rep(max(E_target + rho_lift, floor_epsZ), length(Zobs))
  } else {
    s   <- pmin(Zobs / T_cap_Z, 1)        # scale-free index in [0,1]
    psi <- (1 - s)^4 * (1 + 4*s)          # Wendland C^2 lift
    epsZ_core <- E_target * log1p(k * s) / log1p(k) + rho_lift * psi
    epsZ <- pmax(epsZ_core, floor_epsZ)
  }

  # Map back to T-scale -------------------------------------------------------
  epsT <- epsZ * sig_ok
  epsT
}

```

## Helpers: listing files, parsing settings, estimator runner

```{r helpers}
# Root where generated data are stored
path_data <- path_source("01_data_gen")

path_acc <- file.path(path_data, "sim_accuracy_data")
path_err <- file.path(path_data, "sim_error_data")

stopifnot(dir.exists(path_acc), dir.exists(path_err))

parse_acc_file <- function(fn) {
  # sim_acc_n{n}_d{d}_nperm{nperm}.rds
  m <- stringr::str_match(basename(fn), 
                          "^sim_acc_n(\\d+)_d([0-9.]+)_nperm(\\d+)\\.rds$")
  tibble(n = as.integer(m[,2]),
         d = as.numeric(m[,3]),
         n_perm = as.integer(m[,4]),
         file = fn)
}

parse_err_file <- function(fn) {
  # sim_err_n{n}_d{d}_nperm{nperm}_pi1{pi1}.rds
  m <- stringr::str_match(basename(fn), 
                          "^sim_err_n(\\d+)_d([0-9.]+)_nperm(\\d+)_pi1([0-9.]+)\\.rds$")
  tibble(n = as.integer(m[,2]),
         d = as.numeric(m[,3]),
         n_perm = as.integer(m[,4]),
         pi1 = as.numeric(m[,5]),
         file = fn)
}

list_acc <- function() {
  fs <- list.files(path_acc, pattern = "\\.rds$", full.names = TRUE)
  purrr::map_dfr(fs, parse_acc_file) %>% arrange(n, d, n_perm)
}

list_err <- function() {
  fs <- list.files(path_err, pattern = "\\.rds$", full.names = TRUE)
  purrr::map_dfr(fs, parse_err_file) %>% arrange(n, d, n_perm, pi1)
}

# Build gpd control for (un)constrained
make_ctrl <- function(constraint = c("unconstrained","support_at_obs", 
                                     "support_at_max")) {
  constraint <- match.arg(constraint)
  make_gpd_ctrl(
    fit_method   = "LME",
    include_obs  = FALSE,
    constraint   = constraint,
    thresh_method= "ftr",
    exceed0      = 0.25,
    exceed_min   = 10,
    thresh_step  = 10,
    gof_test     = "ad",
    gof_alpha    = 0.05,
    cores        = 1,
    verbose      = FALSE
  )
}

# Compute p-values for one *panel matrix* (n_test x (n_perm+2))
# Columns 1:n_perm = perm t's, "tobs", "pttest"
estimate_panel <- function(panel, n, d, n_perm, study, pi1 = NA_real_) {
  n_test <- nrow(panel)
  stopifnot(ncol(panel) == n_perm + 2)
  colnames(panel) <- c(as.character(seq_len(n_perm)), "tobs", "pttest")

  # Controls (only keep the ones we actually use)
  ctrl_un  <- make_ctrl("unconstrained")
  ctrl_con <- make_ctrl("support_at_obs")

  # Prepare full inputs (vectorized across tests)
  # perm matrix shape must be B x m (columns = tests)
  perm_mat <- t(as.matrix(panel[, seq_len(n_perm), drop = FALSE]))  # (n_perm x n_test)
  tobs_vec <- as.numeric(panel[, "tobs"])
  ptt_vec  <- as.numeric(panel[, "pttest"])

  # Optional truth labels for error runs
  truth_vec <- if (is.finite(pi1) && !is.na(pi1)) {
    n_alt <- round(n_test * pi1)
    factor(c(rep("alt", n_alt), rep("null", n_test - n_alt)), levels = c("null","alt"))
  } else {
    factor(rep(NA_character_, n_test), levels = c("null","alt"))
  }

  # Unconstrained GPD
  res_un <- compute_p_values(
    obs_stats    = tobs_vec,
    perm_stats   = perm_mat,
    alternative  = "two_sided",
    method       = "gpd",
    fit_thresh   = 1,
    gpd_ctrl     = ctrl_un,
    adjust_method= "none"
  )

  # Constrained with SLLS rule
  res_slls <- compute_p_values(
    obs_stats    = tobs_vec,
    perm_stats   = perm_mat,
    eps_fct      = eps_slls,
    sampsize     = n,
    alternative  = "two_sided",
    method       = "gpd",
    fit_thresh   = 1,
    gpd_ctrl     = ctrl_con,
    adjust_method= "none"
  )

  tibble(
    study      = study,
    n          = n,
    d          = d,
    n_perm     = n_perm,
    pi1        = pi1,
    test_idx   = seq_len(n_test),
    truth      = truth_vec,
    p_ttest    = ptt_vec,
    p_gpd_un   = res_un$p_unadjusted,
    p_gpd_slls = res_slls$p_unadjusted,
    meth_un    = res_un$method_used,
    meth_slls  = res_slls$method_used
  )
}
```

## ACCURACY

### Compute p-values and save

```{r accuracy-run, eval=FALSE}
acc_grid <- list_acc()
handlers("txtprogressbar")
plan(multisession, workers = cores)

acc_out_dir <- path_target("acc_results")
dir.create(acc_out_dir, showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE)

with_progress({
  p <- progressor(steps = nrow(acc_grid))

  furrr::future_walk(
    split(acc_grid, seq_len(nrow(acc_grid))),
    function(row) {
      row <- as_tibble(row)
      n      <- row$n[[1]]
      d      <- row$d[[1]]
      n_perm <- row$n_perm[[1]]
      f_in   <- row$file[[1]]

      f_out  <- file.path(acc_out_dir,
                          sprintf("acc_results_pvals_n%d_d%.1f_nperm%d.rds", 
                                  n, d, n_perm))
      if (file.exists(f_out)) { p(); return(invisible()) }

      # each RDS: list of length n_rep; each element: matrix [n_test x (n_perm+2)]
      lst <- readRDS(f_in)

      res <- purrr::imap_dfr(lst, function(panel, rep_id) {
        estimate_panel(panel, n=n, d=d, n_perm=n_perm, study="accuracy") %>%
          mutate(rep = rep_id)
      })

      saveRDS(res, f_out)
      p()
    },
    .options = furrr::furrr_options(seed = TRUE)
  )
})

plan(sequential)
```

### Collect & reshape

```{r accuracy-collect}
acc_files <- list.files(path_target("acc_results"), 
                        pattern="\\.rds$", full.names = TRUE)
acc_res   <- purrr::map_dfr(acc_files, readRDS)

acc_long <- acc_res %>%
  select(study, n, d, n_perm, rep, test_idx, 
         p_ttest, p_gpd_un, p_gpd_slls) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(p_ttest, p_gpd_un, p_gpd_slls),
    names_to = "method", values_to = "pval"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    method = factor(method,
      levels = c("p_ttest","p_gpd_un", "p_gpd_slls"),
      labels = c("t-test","GPD(U)", "GPD(C,SLLS)")
    )
  )
```

```{r colors}
my_cols <- c(
  "t-test"          = "gray40",
  "GPD(U)"          = "#56B4E9",  
  "GPD(C,SLLS)"     = "orange2",  
  "GPD(C)"  = "#F8766D"
)

theme_params <- list(
  axis.text       = element_text(size = 12),
  axis.text.x     = element_text(angle = 0, hjust = 0.5),
  axis.title      = element_text(size = 16),
  legend.text     = element_text(size = 14),
  legend.title    = element_text(size = 16),
  plot.title      = element_text(size = 16)
)
```

### P-values by n (boxplots only)

```{r}
## Prepare data frame for plotting (global zero replacement)

# Smallest nonzero p across everything shown in the plot
min_nonzero_global <- suppressWarnings(min(acc_long$pval[acc_long$pval > 0], na.rm = TRUE))
if (!is.finite(min_nonzero_global)) min_nonzero_global <- 1e-320

# Choose a floor (a few orders smaller than the min nonzero)
p_floor <- min_nonzero_global * 1e-3

acc_long <- acc_long %>%
  mutate(pval_plot = ifelse(pval == 0, p_floor, pval))

# Y-axis breaks + label "0" at the floor
base_breaks <- c(1e-1, 10^seq(-20, -300, by = -20))
yrange_max  <- max(acc_long$pval_plot, na.rm = TRUE)
brks <- sort(unique(c(p_floor, base_breaks[base_breaks <= yrange_max])))
```


```{r accuracy_by_n_boxplot, fig.width = 10, fig.height = 6, dpi = 200}
df_plot <- acc_long %>%
  filter(n_perm == 1000, d == 1)

# Accuracy boxplot using the replaced values
gg_acc <- ggplot(df_plot, aes(x = factor(n), y = pval_plot, fill = method)) +
  geom_boxplot(
    outlier.size = 0.3, width = 0.75,
    position = position_dodge2(preserve = "single", padding = 0.2)
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual("Method", values = my_cols) +
  scale_y_log10(
    breaks = brks,
    labels = function(x) {
      ifelse(x < p_floor * 1.1, "0",
             paste0("1e", log10(x)))
    })+  
  labs(title = "", x = "n", y = expression(p)) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(
    !!!theme_params,
    legend.position = "bottom",
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
  )

gg_acc
```

### P-values by n

```{r}
# Plot function
plot_permapprox <- function(acc_long,
                            type = c("pval", "ratio"),
                            x = c("n", "d", "n_perm"),
                            xlab = NULL,
                            fixed = list(),            # e.g. list(d = 1, n_perm = 1000)
                            my_cols,
                            theme_params = list(),
                            zero_floor_factor = 1e-3,  # p-values: 0 -> min_nonzero * factor
                            y_min = 1e-300,
                            y_max = 1e+300,
                            y_step_size = 20,
                            jitter_width = 0.18,
                            dodge_width = 0.75,
                            show_zero_labels = TRUE,   # annotate "0s=…"
                            legend_point_size = 2.5) {

  stopifnot(all(c("n","d","n_perm","method","pval") %in% names(acc_long)))

  type <- match.arg(type)
  x    <- match.arg(x)

  # ---- Decide needed fixed filters based on x ----
  # default anchors if not provided
  defaults <- list(n = 100, d = 1, n_perm = 1000)
  fixed <- modifyList(defaults, fixed)

  # build filter
  filt <- dplyr::quo(TRUE)
  if (x != "n")      filt <- rlang::expr(!!filt & n      == !!fixed$n)
  if (x != "d")      filt <- rlang::expr(!!filt & d      == !!fixed$d)
  if (x != "n_perm") filt <- rlang::expr(!!filt & n_perm == !!fixed$n_perm)

  df <- acc_long %>%
    dplyr::filter(!!filt)

  if (!nrow(df)) stop("No rows after filtering. Adjust 'fixed = list(...)' to existing settings.")

  # order methods to match colors provided
  df <- df %>%
    dplyr::mutate(method = factor(method, levels = names(my_cols)))

  # helper for legend guides
  guide_bump <- list(
    ggplot2::guide_legend(override.aes = list(size = legend_point_size)),
    ggplot2::guide_legend(override.aes = list(size = legend_point_size))
  )

  # ---------------------------
  # P-VALUE PLOT
  # ---------------------------
  if (type == "pval") {
    # compute global floor for zeros (across the plotted subset)
    min_nonzero <- suppressWarnings(min(df$pval[df$pval > 0], na.rm = TRUE))
    if (!is.finite(min_nonzero)) min_nonzero <- 1e-320
    p_floor <- min_nonzero * zero_floor_factor

    df <- df %>%
      dplyr::mutate(
        pval_plot = dplyr::if_else(pval == 0, p_floor, pval, missing = pval)
      )

    # jitter points: use all values; boxplots: exclude zeros to avoid huge IQR
    df_points <- df
    df_boxes  <- dplyr::filter(df, pval > 0)

    # zero counts per x × method
    zero_counts <- df %>%
      dplyr::group_by(.data[[x]], method) %>%
      dplyr::summarise(n_zero = sum(pval == 0, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
      dplyr::filter(n_zero > 0)

    # y-scale breaks + "0" tick only if there are zeros present
    has_zero <- any(df$pval == 0, na.rm = TRUE)
    # y_min <- min(df$pval_plot, na.rm = TRUE)
    # y_max <- max(df$pval_plot, na.rm = TRUE)
    
    # A tick every 20 powers
    base_breaks <- c(1e-2, 10^seq(-20, -300, by = -y_step_size))
    brks <- base_breaks[base_breaks <= (y_max) & 
                          base_breaks >= (y_min)]
    
    if (has_zero) brks <- sort(unique(c(p_floor, brks)))
    
    y_labs <- function(v) {
      if (has_zero) {
        ifelse(v < p_floor * 1.1, "0", paste0("1e", as.integer(log10(v))))
      } else {
        paste0("1e", as.integer(log10(v)))
      }
    }
    
    p <- ggplot(mapping = aes(x = factor(.data[[x]]))) +
      # jitter (colored)
      geom_point(
        data = df_points,
        aes(y = pval_plot, color = method, fill = method),
        size = 0.9, alpha = 0.9,
        position = position_jitterdodge(jitter.width = jitter_width, 
                                        dodge.width = dodge_width)
      ) +
      # box (transparent outline) on non-zeros only
      geom_boxplot(
        data = df_boxes,
        aes(y = pval_plot, group = interaction(method, .data[[x]])),
        fill = NA, color = "black", width = 0.65,
        outlier.shape = NA, position = position_dodge(width = dodge_width),
        show.legend = FALSE
      ) +
      # annotate zero counts (optional)
      {if (show_zero_labels && nrow(zero_counts)) geom_text(
        data = zero_counts,
        aes(x = factor(.data[[x]]), y = if (length(brks)) min(brks) else p_floor,
                     label = paste0("0s=", n_zero), color = method, group = method),
        position = position_dodge(width = dodge_width),
        vjust = 1.6, size = 3.5, inherit.aes = FALSE, show.legend = FALSE
      )}

    p <- p +
      scale_fill_manual("Method", values = my_cols) +
      scale_color_manual("Method", values = my_cols) +
      scale_y_log10(breaks = brks, labels = y_labs) +
      labs(title = "", 
           x = ifelse(is.null(xlab), x, xlab), 
           y = expression(p)) +
      theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
      do.call(theme, c(theme_params, list(
        legend.position = "bottom",
        panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
      ))) +
      guides(color = guide_bump[[1]], fill = guide_bump[[2]])

    return(p)
  }

  # ---------------------------
  # RATIO PLOT (method / t-test), non-zero pairs only
  # ---------------------------
  # For ratio, we need tests aligned with t-test baseline
  df_base <- df %>%
    dplyr::select(n, d, n_perm, !!rlang::sym(x), rep, test_idx, method, pval)

  # Count zeros for annotation (exclude t-test)
  zero_counts_r <- df_base %>%
    dplyr::filter(method != "t-test") %>%
    dplyr::group_by(.data[[x]], method) %>%
    dplyr::summarise(n_zero = sum(pval == 0, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
    dplyr::filter(n_zero > 0)

  # Wide join to get t-test next to each method
  df_wide <- tidyr::pivot_wider(df_base, names_from = method, values_from = pval)

  methods_to_compare <- intersect(names(my_cols), setdiff(unique(acc_long$method), "t-test"))
  if (length(methods_to_compare) == 0) {
    methods_to_compare <- c("GPD(U)", "GPD(C,f=0.05)", "GPD(C,SLLS)") # fallback
  }

  df_ratio <- df_wide %>%
    tidyr::pivot_longer(cols = tidyselect::all_of(methods_to_compare),
                        names_to = "method", values_to = "p_method") %>%
    dplyr::rename(p_ttest = `t-test`) %>%
    dplyr::filter(p_ttest > 0, p_method > 0) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(
      method = factor(method, levels = names(my_cols)),
      ratio  = p_method / p_ttest
    )
  
  if (!nrow(df_ratio)) 
    stop("No non-zero pairs for ratio plot after filtering. Try a different 'fixed' combination.")
  
  r_min <- min(df_ratio$ratio, na.rm = TRUE)
  r_max <- max(df_ratio$ratio, na.rm = TRUE)
  
  base_breaks <- sort(c(10^seq(300, -300, by = -y_step_size)))
  # brks_r <- base_breaks[base_breaks <= (r_max * 10^y_step_size) & 
  #                         base_breaks >= (r_min * 10^-y_step_size)]
  brks_r <- base_breaks[base_breaks <= y_max & base_breaks >= y_min]
  
  lab_plain <- function(v) ifelse(v == 1, "1", paste0("1e", as.integer(log10(v))))

  p <- ggplot(mapping = aes(x = factor(.data[[x]]))) +
    geom_hline(yintercept = 1, linewidth = 0.5, linetype = 2, color = "grey50") +
    geom_point(
      data = df_ratio,
      aes(y = ratio, color = method, fill = method),
      size = 0.9, alpha = 0.9,
      position = position_jitterdodge(jitter.width = jitter_width, dodge.width = dodge_width)
    ) +
    geom_boxplot(
      data = df_ratio,
      aes(y = ratio, group = interaction(method, .data[[x]])),
      fill = NA, color = "black", width = 0.65,
      outlier.shape = NA, position = position_dodge(width = dodge_width),
      show.legend = FALSE
    ) +
    {if (show_zero_labels && nrow(zero_counts_r)) geom_text(
      data = zero_counts_r,
      aes(x = factor(.data[[x]]), y = min(brks_r),
                   label = paste0("0s=", n_zero),
                   color = method, group = method),
      position = position_dodge(width = dodge_width),
      vjust = 1.6, size = 3.5, inherit.aes = FALSE, show.legend = FALSE
    )} +
    scale_fill_manual("Method", values = my_cols) +
    scale_color_manual("Method", values = my_cols) +
    scale_y_log10(breaks = brks_r, labels = lab_plain) +
    labs(title = "", 
         x = ifelse(is.null(xlab), x, xlab), 
         y = expression(p[method] / p[ttest])) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
    do.call(theme, c(theme_params, list(
      legend.position = "bottom",
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
    ))) +
    guides(color = guide_bump[[1]], fill = guide_bump[[2]])

  return(p)
}
```


Approximated p-values with sample size on the x axis. The boxplots represent non-zero p-values only.

```{r accuracy_pval_by_n_boxplot, fig.width = 10, fig.height = 6, dpi = 200}
# P-values by n (fix d=1, B=1000)
plot_permapprox(acc_long, type = "pval", x = "n",
                fixed = list(d = 1, n_perm = 1000),
                my_cols = my_cols, theme_params = theme_params,
                y_min = 1e-130, y_max = 1)
```

### Ratios by n

Ratios ($p_{method}$ / $p_{ttest}$) for non-zero p-values, with sample size on the x axis.

```{r accuracy_ratio_by_n_boxplot, fig.width = 10, fig.height = 6, dpi = 200}
# P-values by n (fix d=1, B=1000)
plot_permapprox(acc_long, type = "ratio", x = "n",
                fixed = list(d = 1, n_perm = 1000),
                my_cols = my_cols, theme_params = theme_params,
                y_min = 1e-40)
```


### P-values by d

Approximated p-values with effect size on the x axis. The boxplots represent non-zero p-values only.

```{r accuracy_pval_by_d_boxplot, fig.width = 10, fig.height = 6, dpi = 200}
plot_permapprox(acc_long, type = "pval", x = "d",
                fixed = list(d = 1, n_perm = 1000),
                my_cols = my_cols, theme_params = theme_params,
                y_min = 1e-50)
```

### Ratios by d

Ratios ($p_{method}$ / $p_{ttest}$) for non-zero p-values, with effect size on the x axis.

```{r accuracy_ratio_by_d_boxplot, fig.width = 10, fig.height = 6, dpi = 200}
plot_permapprox(acc_long, type = "ratio", x = "d",
                fixed = list(n = 100, n_perm = 1000),
                my_cols = my_cols, theme_params = theme_params,
                y_step_size = 5, y_min = 1e-20)
```

### P-values by B

Approximated p-values with number of permutations on the x axis. The boxplots represent non-zero p-values only.

```{r accuracy_pval_by_B_boxplot, fig.width = 10, fig.height = 6, dpi = 200}
plot_permapprox(acc_long, type = "pval", x = "n_perm",
                fixed = list(d = 1, n = 100),
                my_cols = my_cols, theme_params = theme_params,
                y_step_size = 10, y_min = 1e-30)
```

### Ratios by B

Ratios ($p_{method}$ / $p_{ttest}$) for non-zero p-values, with number of permutations on the x axis.

```{r accuracy_ratio_by_B_boxplot, fig.width = 10, fig.height = 6, dpi = 200}
plot_permapprox(acc_long, type = "ratio", x = "n_perm",
                fixed = list(d = 1, n = 100),
                my_cols = my_cols, theme_params = theme_params,
                y_step_size = 5, y_min = 1e-30)
```

### Table: Zeros and times where method p-value < t-test p-value

```{r}
df_base <- acc_long %>%
  filter(n_perm == 1000, d == 1) %>%
  select(n, rep, test_idx, method, pval)

# wide: get t-test alongside each method's p
df_wide <- df_base %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = method, values_from = pval)

# methods to compare
method_levels <- c("GPD(U)", "GPD(C,SLLS)")

# long with comparison & indicators
cmp_long <- df_wide %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = all_of(method_levels),
               names_to = "method", values_to = "p_method") %>%
  rename(p_ttest = `t-test`) %>%
  mutate(
    is_zero   = p_method == 0,
    below_tt  = p_method < p_ttest
  )

# summary table
tab_zero_below <- cmp_long %>%
  group_by(n, method) %>%
  summarise(
    zeros       = sum(is_zero,   na.rm = TRUE),
    below_ttest = sum(below_tt,  na.rm = TRUE),
    total       = sum(!is.na(p_method) & !is.na(p_ttest)),
    pct_zeros        = scales::percent(zeros / pmax(total, 1), accuracy = 0.1),
    pct_below_ttest  = scales::percent(below_ttest / pmax(total, 1), accuracy = 0.1),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  arrange(n, factor(method, levels = method_levels)) %>%
  select(n, method, zeros, pct_zeros, below_ttest, pct_below_ttest, total) %>%
  rename('zeros (%)' = pct_zeros,
         '< t-test' = below_ttest,
         '< t-test (%)' = pct_below_ttest
         )

# print nicely
tab_zero_below %>%
  kableExtra::kable()
```

### Table: Accuracy metrics

```{r}
# --- Accuracy metrics helper (t-test = ground truth) --------------------------
# data_methods: data frame with columns n,d,n_perm,rep,test_idx,method,pval
# baseline: (optional) rows containing the t-test baseline with the same keys.
#           If NULL, the function tries to find baseline inside data_methods.
acc_metrics_summary <- function(data_methods,
                                group_vars = c("n","method"),
                                baseline = NULL) {

  keys <- c("n","d","n_perm","rep","test_idx")
  stopifnot(all(c(keys,"method","pval") %in% names(data_methods)))

  # Prepare/locate baseline (t-test)
  if (is.null(baseline)) {
    baseline <- data_methods %>% dplyr::filter(method == "t-test")
    if (nrow(baseline) == 0L)
      stop("No t-test baseline found. Pass it via `baseline=` or include it in `data_methods`.")
  } else {
    stopifnot(all(c(keys,"pval") %in% names(baseline)))
  }

  # Methods (exclude t-test)
  df_m <- data_methods %>%
    dplyr::filter(method != "t-test") %>%
    dplyr::select(dplyr::all_of(keys), method, p_method = pval)

  # Baseline
  df_t <- baseline %>%
    dplyr::select(dplyr::all_of(keys), p_ttest = pval)

  # Pair up by keys
  long <- dplyr::inner_join(df_m, df_t, by = keys) %>%
    dplyr::filter(is.finite(p_method), is.finite(p_ttest)) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(diff = p_method - p_ttest)

  # Ratios on strictly positive pairs
  long_pos <- long %>%
    dplyr::filter(p_method > 0, p_ttest > 0) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(log10_ratio = log10(p_method / p_ttest))

  sum_core <- long %>%
    dplyr::group_by(dplyr::across(dplyr::all_of(group_vars))) %>%
    dplyr::summarise(
      N         = dplyr::n(),
      bias      = mean(diff),
      sd        = stats::sd(diff),
      rmse      = sqrt(mean(diff^2)),
      cor_spear = suppressWarnings(stats::cor(p_method, p_ttest, method = "spearman")),
      .groups   = "drop"
    )

  sum_ratio <- long_pos %>%
    dplyr::group_by(dplyr::across(dplyr::all_of(group_vars))) %>%
    dplyr::summarise(
      N_pos          = dplyr::n(),
      .groups = "drop"
    )

  dplyr::left_join(sum_core, sum_ratio, by = group_vars)
}
```


```{r accuracy-metrics-by-n}
# setting: d=1, B=1000
acc_for_metrics <- acc_long %>% dplyr::filter(d == 1, n_perm == 1000)

tab_metrics_by_n <- acc_metrics_summary(
  data_methods = acc_for_metrics,
  group_vars   = c("n","method")
) %>% dplyr::arrange(n, method)

tab_metrics_by_n_fmt <- tab_metrics_by_n %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    dplyr::across(
      c(bias, sd, rmse),
      ~ format(.x, scientific = TRUE, digits = 3)
    ),
    dplyr::across(
      c(cor_spear),
      ~ format(round(.x, 3), nsmall = 3)
    )
  )

knitr::kable(tab_metrics_by_n_fmt,
  caption = "Accuracy vs. t-test by n (d=1, B=1000).") %>%
  kableExtra::kable_classic(full_width = FALSE)
```

```{r accuracy-metrics-all}
tab_metrics_all <- acc_metrics_summary(
  data_methods = acc_long,                    # contains t-test too
  group_vars   = c("n","d","n_perm","method")
) %>% dplyr::arrange(n, d, n_perm, method)

tab_metrics_all_fmt <- tab_metrics_all %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    dplyr::across(
      c(bias, sd, rmse),
      ~ format(.x, scientific = TRUE, digits = 3)
    ),
    dplyr::across(
      c(cor_spear),
      ~ format(round(.x, 3), nsmall = 3)
    )
  )

knitr::kable(tab_metrics_all_fmt,
  caption = "Accuracy vs. t-test across all settings.") %>%
  kableExtra::kable_classic(full_width = FALSE)
```

### Correlation plot (t-test vs. methods)

```{r corr_scatter_n100_d1_B1000, fig.width=10, fig.height=5, dpi=200}
# Filter the setting
df_set <- acc_long %>%
  dplyr::filter(n == 100, d == 1, n_perm == 1000) %>%
  dplyr::select(n, d, n_perm, rep, test_idx, method, pval)

# Wide join to get baseline next to methods
df_wide <- df_set %>%
  tidyr::pivot_wider(names_from = method, values_from = pval)

# Reshape to long with two methods vs baseline
methods_to_plot <- c("GPD(U)", "GPD(C,SLLS)")
df_long <- df_wide %>%
  tidyr::pivot_longer(cols = tidyselect::all_of(methods_to_plot),
                      names_to = "method", values_to = "p_method") %>%
  dplyr::rename(p_ttest = `t-test`) %>%
  dplyr::filter(!is.na(p_ttest), !is.na(p_method)) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(method = factor(method, levels = methods_to_plot))

# Correlations on strictly positive pairs (no zero replacement here)
corr_tab <- df_long %>%
  dplyr::filter(p_ttest > 0, p_method > 0) %>%
  dplyr::group_by(method) %>%
  dplyr::summarise(
    pearson  = suppressWarnings(stats::cor(p_method, p_ttest, method = "pearson")),
    spearman = suppressWarnings(stats::cor(p_method, p_ttest, method = "spearman")),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# Prepare plotting values (replace zeros by floor ONLY for plotting)
min_pos <- suppressWarnings(min(c(df_long$p_ttest[df_long$p_ttest > 0],
                                  df_long$p_method[df_long$p_method > 0]), na.rm = TRUE))
if (!is.finite(min_pos)) min_pos <- 1e-320
p_floor <- min_pos * 1e-3

df_plot <- df_long %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    p_ttest_plot  = dplyr::if_else(p_ttest  > 0, p_ttest,  p_floor),
    p_method_plot = dplyr::if_else(p_method > 0, p_method, p_floor)
  )

# Subtitle with correlations
sub_txt <- corr_tab %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    pearson  = format(round(pearson,  3), nsmall = 3),
    spearman = format(round(spearman, 3), nsmall = 3),
    label = paste0(method, ": r=", pearson, ", r_s=", spearman)
  ) %>%
  dplyr::pull(label) %>%
  paste(collapse = "   |   ")

# Colors to match your palette
corr_cols <- c("GPD(U)" = my_cols[["GPD(U)"]], "GPD(C,SLLS)" = my_cols[["GPD(C,SLLS)"]])

ggplot(df_plot, aes(x = p_ttest_plot, y = p_method_plot, color = method)) +
  geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, linetype = 2, linewidth = 0.5, color = "grey50") +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.6, size = 0.8) +
  scale_color_manual(values = corr_cols, name = "Method") +
  scale_x_log10() +
  scale_y_log10() +
  labs(
    title = "Correlation of approximated p-values vs. t-test (n=100, d=1, B=1000)",
    subtitle = sub_txt,
    x = expression(paste("t-test ", p)),
    y = expression(paste(p, " (method)"))
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "bottom",
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
  )

```




## ERROR RATES

### Compute p-values and save

```{r error-run, eval=FALSE}
# Grid from generated files
err_grid <- list_err()

handlers("txtprogressbar")
plan(multisession, workers = cores)

err_out_dir <- path_target("err_results")
dir.create(err_out_dir, showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE)

with_progress({
  p <- progressor(steps = nrow(err_grid))

  furrr::future_walk(
    split(err_grid, seq_len(nrow(err_grid))),
    function(row) {
      row <- as_tibble(row)
      n      <- row$n[[1]]
      d      <- row$d[[1]]
      n_perm <- row$n_perm[[1]]
      pi1    <- row$pi1[[1]]
      f_in   <- row$file[[1]]

      f_out <- file.path(
        err_out_dir,
        sprintf("err_results_pvals_n%d_d%.1f_nperm%d_pi1%.2f.rds",
                n, d, n_perm, pi1)
      )
      if (file.exists(f_out)) { p(); return(invisible()) }

      # Each RDS is a list of length n_rep; element: matrix [n_test x (n_perm+2)]
      lst <- readRDS(f_in)

      res <- purrr::imap_dfr(lst, function(panel, rep_id) {
        estimate_panel(panel, n=n, d=d, n_perm=n_perm, pi1=pi1, study="error") %>%
          mutate(rep = rep_id)
      })

      saveRDS(res, f_out)
      p()
    },
    .options = furrr::furrr_options(seed = TRUE)
  )
})

plan(sequential)
```

### Collect & reshape

```{r}
# Collect all error panels' results
err_files <- list.files(path_target("err_results"), 
                        pattern="\\.rds$", full.names = TRUE)
err_res   <- purrr::map_dfr(err_files, readRDS)

# Long format with method labels for downstream metrics/plots
err_long <- err_res %>%
  select(study, n, d, n_perm, pi1, rep, test_idx, truth, 
         p_ttest, p_gpd_un, p_gpd_slls) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(p_ttest, p_gpd_un, p_gpd_slls),
    names_to = "method", values_to = "pval"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    method = factor(method,
      levels = c("p_ttest","p_gpd_un", "p_gpd_slls"),
      labels = c("t-test","GPD(U)", "GPD(C,SLLS)")
    )
  )

my_cols <- c(
  "t-test"          = "gray40",
  "GPD(U)"          = "#56B4E9",  
  "GPD(C,SLLS)"     = "orange2",  
  "GPD(C)"  = "#F8766D"
)

theme_params <- list(
  axis.text       = element_text(size = 12),
  axis.text.x     = element_text(angle = 0, hjust = 0.5),
  axis.title      = element_text(size = 16),
  legend.text     = element_text(size = 14),
  legend.title    = element_text(size = 16),
  plot.title      = element_text(size = 16)
)
```

### Compute unadjusted & adjusted metrics

```{r}
# Helper: compute BH-adjusted p-values per (setting × replicate × method)
err_long_adj <- err_long %>%
  group_by(n, d, n_perm, pi1, rep, method) %>%
  mutate(pval_bh = p.adjust(pval, method = "BH")) %>%
  ungroup()

# Metric engine ---------------------------------------------------------------
# Returns tidy rows per alpha with: rejection_rate, false_positive_rate, power,
# FDR_unadj, FDR_adj
compute_metrics <- function(df, alphas = c(0.05, 0.01)) {
  # df: rows from a single (n,d,n_perm,pi1,rep,method) group with pval and pval_bh
  stopifnot(all(c("pval","pval_bh","truth") %in% names(df)))
  m0 <- sum(df$truth == "null", na.rm = TRUE)
  m1 <- sum(df$truth == "alt",  na.rm = TRUE)
  out <- map_dfr(alphas, function(a) {
    # Unadjusted decisions
    dec_u  <- df$pval <= a
    R_u    <- sum(dec_u, na.rm = TRUE)
    FP_u   <- sum(dec_u & df$truth == "null", na.rm = TRUE)
    TP_u   <- sum(dec_u & df$truth == "alt",  na.rm = TRUE)

    # BH-adjusted decisions
    dec_bh <- df$pval_bh <= a
    R_bh   <- sum(dec_bh, na.rm = TRUE)
    FP_bh  <- sum(dec_bh & df$truth == "null", na.rm = TRUE)
    TP_bh  <- sum(dec_bh & df$truth == "alt",  na.rm = TRUE)

    tibble(
      alpha               = a,
      rejection_rate      = R_u / (m0 + m1),
      false_positive_rate = if (m0 > 0) FP_u / m0 else NA_real_,
      power               = if (m1 > 0) TP_u / m1 else NA_real_,
      FDR_unadjusted      = if (R_u > 0) FP_u / R_u else 0,
      FDR_adjusted        = if (R_bh > 0) FP_bh / R_bh else 0
    )
  })
  out
}

# Summarize metrics per replicate and then aggregate (mean & 95% CI) ----------
metrics_by_rep <- err_long_adj %>%
  group_by(n, d, n_perm, pi1, rep, method) %>%
  group_modify(~ compute_metrics(.x)) %>%
  ungroup()

summary_metrics <- metrics_by_rep %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = c(rejection_rate, false_positive_rate, power,
                        FDR_unadjusted, FDR_adjusted),
               names_to = "metric", values_to = "value") %>%
  group_by(n, d, n_perm, pi1, method, alpha, metric) %>%
  summarise(
    mean = mean(value, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd   = sd(value,   na.rm = TRUE),
    nrep = sum(is.finite(value)),
    se   = sd / sqrt(pmax(nrep, 1)),
    lo95 = pmax(0, mean - 1.96 * se),
    hi95 = pmin(1, mean + 1.96 * se),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    metric_label = case_when(
      metric == "false_positive_rate" ~ "False positive rate",
      metric == "FDR_adjusted" ~ "FDR (adjusted)",
      metric == "FDR_unadjusted" ~ "FDR (unadjusted)",
      metric == "power" ~ "Power",
      metric == "rejection_rate" ~ "Rejection rate"
    )
  )
```

### Flexible plotting helper

```{r}
plot_error_metric <- function(summary_metrics,
                              metric = c("rejection_rate","false_positive_rate",
                                         "power","FDR_unadjusted","FDR_adjusted"),
                              x = c("pi1","n","d","n_perm"),
                              fixed = list(),          # e.g. list(n = 100, d = 1, n_perm = 1000)
                              alpha = 0.05,
                              my_cols = my_cols,
                              ylim = c(0, 1), 
                              theme_params = list()) {
  
  metric <- match.arg(metric, c("rejection_rate","false_positive_rate",
                                "power","FDR_unadjusted","FDR_adjusted"))
  x      <- match.arg(x, choices = c("pi1","n","d","n_perm"))
  
  # Defaults for fixed filters (anchor the non-x dims)
  defaults <- list(n = 100, d = 1, n_perm = 1000, pi1 = unique(summary_metrics$pi1)[1])
  fixed <- modifyList(defaults, fixed)

  filt <- quo(TRUE)
  if (x != "n")      filt <- expr(!!filt & n      == !!fixed$n)
  if (x != "d")      filt <- expr(!!filt & d      == !!fixed$d)
  if (x != "n_perm") filt <- expr(!!filt & n_perm == !!fixed$n_perm)
  if (x != "pi1")    filt <- expr(!!filt & abs(pi1 - !!fixed$pi1) < 1e-12)

  df <- summary_metrics %>%
    filter(metric == !!metric, alpha == !!alpha) %>%
    filter(!!filt) %>%
    mutate(method = factor(method, levels = names(my_cols)))

  if (!nrow(df)) stop("No data for the chosen filters.")
  
  metric_label <- as.character(df[1, "metric_label"])
  
  p <- ggplot(df, aes(x = as.factor(.data[[x]]), y = mean, color = method, group = method))
    #geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linewidth = 0.3, linetype = 2)
  
  if (metric %in% c("false_positive_rate", "FDR_adjusted")) {
    p <- p + geom_hline(yintercept = 0.05, linewidth = 0.3, linetype = 2)
  }
  
  p <- p + geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = lo95, ymax = hi95, fill = method),
                       alpha = 0.15, color = NA, show.legend = FALSE) +
    geom_line() +
    geom_point(size = 1.6) +
    scale_color_manual("Method", values = my_cols) +
    scale_fill_manual(values = my_cols) +
    scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 1), breaks = scales::pretty_breaks(6)) +
    labs(x = x, y = metric_label, title = paste0(metric_label, " (alpha = ", alpha, ")")) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
    coord_cartesian(ylim = ylim) +
    do.call(theme, c(theme_params, list(
      legend.position = "bottom",
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
    )))
  p
}
```


### By non-null fraction (pi1)

```{r}
# By non-null fraction (recommended primary view)
plot_error_metric(summary_metrics, metric = "rejection_rate",
                  x = "pi1", fixed = list(n = 100, d = 1, n_perm = 1000),
                  alpha = 0.05, my_cols = my_cols, theme_params = theme_params,
                  ylim = c(0, 0.2))

plot_error_metric(summary_metrics, metric = "false_positive_rate",
                  x = "pi1", fixed = list(n = 100, d = 1, n_perm = 1000),
                  alpha = 0.05, my_cols = my_cols, theme_params = theme_params,
                  ylim = c(0.04, 0.06))

plot_error_metric(summary_metrics, metric = "power",
                  x = "pi1", fixed = list(n = 100, d = 1, n_perm = 1000),
                  alpha = 0.05, my_cols = my_cols, theme_params = theme_params,
                  ylim = c(0.9, 1))

plot_error_metric(summary_metrics, metric = "FDR_unadjusted",
                  x = "pi1", fixed = list(n = 100, d = 1, n_perm = 1000),
                  alpha = 0.05, my_cols = my_cols, theme_params = theme_params)

plot_error_metric(summary_metrics, metric = "FDR_adjusted",
                  x = "pi1", fixed = list(n = 100, d = 1, n_perm = 1000),
                  alpha = 0.05, my_cols = my_cols, theme_params = theme_params)
```
### By sample size (n)

```{r}
# By non-null fraction (recommended primary view)
plot_error_metric(summary_metrics, metric = "rejection_rate",
                  x = "n", fixed = list(pi1 = 0.05, d = 1, n_perm = 1000),
                  alpha = 0.05, my_cols = my_cols, theme_params = theme_params,
                  ylim = c(0, 0.2))

plot_error_metric(summary_metrics, metric = "false_positive_rate",
                  x = "n", fixed = list(pi1 = 0.05, d = 1, n_perm = 1000),
                  alpha = 0.05, my_cols = my_cols, theme_params = theme_params,
                  ylim = c(0.04, 0.06))

plot_error_metric(summary_metrics, metric = "power",
                  x = "n", fixed = list(pi1 = 0.05, d = 1, n_perm = 1000),
                  alpha = 0.05, my_cols = my_cols, theme_params = theme_params,
                  ylim = c(0.9, 1))

plot_error_metric(summary_metrics, metric = "FDR_unadjusted",
                  x = "n", fixed = list(pi1 = 0.05, d = 1, n_perm = 1000),
                  alpha = 0.05, my_cols = my_cols, theme_params = theme_params)

plot_error_metric(summary_metrics, metric = "FDR_adjusted",
                  x = "n", fixed = list(pi1 = 0.05, d = 1, n_perm = 1000),
                  alpha = 0.05, my_cols = my_cols, theme_params = theme_params,
                  ylim = c(0, 0.2))
```

### By effect size (d)

```{r}
# By non-null fraction (recommended primary view)
plot_error_metric(summary_metrics, metric = "rejection_rate",
                  x = "d", fixed = list(n = 100, pi1 = 0.05, n_perm = 1000),
                  alpha = 0.05, my_cols = my_cols, theme_params = theme_params,
                  ylim = c(0, 0.2))

plot_error_metric(summary_metrics, metric = "false_positive_rate",
                  x = "d", fixed = list(n = 100, pi1 = 0.05, n_perm = 1000),
                  alpha = 0.05, my_cols = my_cols, theme_params = theme_params,
                  ylim = c(0.04, 0.06))

plot_error_metric(summary_metrics, metric = "power",
                  x = "d", fixed = list(n = 100, pi1 = 0.05, n_perm = 1000),
                  alpha = 0.05, my_cols = my_cols, theme_params = theme_params,
                  ylim = c(0.9, 1))

plot_error_metric(summary_metrics, metric = "FDR_unadjusted",
                  x = "d", fixed = list(n = 100, pi1 = 0.05, n_perm = 1000),
                  alpha = 0.05, my_cols = my_cols, theme_params = theme_params)

plot_error_metric(summary_metrics, metric = "FDR_adjusted",
                  x = "d", fixed = list(n = 100, pi1 = 0.05, n_perm = 1000),
                  alpha = 0.05, my_cols = my_cols, theme_params = theme_params,
                  ylim = c(0, 0.2))
```

### By number of permutations

```{r}
# By non-null fraction (recommended primary view)
plot_error_metric(summary_metrics, metric = "rejection_rate",
                  x = "n_perm", fixed = list(n = 100, d = 1, pi1 = 0.05),
                  alpha = 0.05, my_cols = my_cols, theme_params = theme_params,
                  ylim = c(0, 0.2))

plot_error_metric(summary_metrics, metric = "false_positive_rate",
                  x = "n_perm", fixed = list(n = 100, d = 1, pi1 = 0.05),
                  alpha = 0.05, my_cols = my_cols, theme_params = theme_params,
                  ylim = c(0.04, 0.06))

plot_error_metric(summary_metrics, metric = "power",
                  x = "n_perm", fixed = list(n = 100, d = 1, pi1 = 0.05),
                  alpha = 0.05, my_cols = my_cols, theme_params = theme_params,
                  ylim = c(0.9, 1))

plot_error_metric(summary_metrics, metric = "FDR_unadjusted",
                  x = "n_perm", fixed = list(n = 100, d = 1, pi1 = 0.05),
                  alpha = 0.05, my_cols = my_cols, theme_params = theme_params)

plot_error_metric(summary_metrics, metric = "FDR_adjusted",
                  x = "n_perm", fixed = list(n = 100, d = 1, pi1 = 0.05),
                  alpha = 0.01, my_cols = my_cols, theme_params = theme_params,
                  ylim = c(0, 0.2))
```


## Files written

These files have been written to the target directory, ```r paste0("data/", params$name)```:

```{r list-files-target}
projthis::proj_dir_info(path_target())
```
